mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for future equity cash flows, mechanically depressing S&P 500 valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS rises, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 5.0%, or t-statistic of return difference vs baseline falls below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher borrowing costs, generating positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of tightening periods, or regime-conditional alpha decays to zero within 12 months of signal activation.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL exceeds 195.0 while FEDFUNDS remains below 3.0%, markets anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, causing sharp negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of trigger events, or the joint condition fails to predict return direction with a log-loss improvement > 0.05 over baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate profit margins, mechanically supporting higher S&P 500 valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods with positive PAYEMS growth, or rolling OLS slope coefficient turns negative over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real GDP growth and retail sales during a late-cycle expansion signals weakening consumer demand relative to output, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence condition produces positive 20-day returns in >50% of cases, or the predictive R-squared of the divergence metric falls below 0.01 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and housing starts below their 12-month moving averages signals a broad supply-demand mismatch that triggers immediate negative market repricing within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of trigger events, or the dual-contraction signal fails to achieve a Sharpe ratio > 0.5 in a long-short portfolio over 3 years.
